title: Open4Gene: a hurdle model-based method for peak-to-gene linkage analysis
author: Hongbo Liu
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Open4Gene (v1.0.0) is a hurdle model-based method for peak-to-gene linkage analysis in the single cell mutiome dataset. 
 Main features of Open4Gene - Accounting for excess zeros in single-nucleus RNA data based on a two-component mixture Hurdle model - Modeling linkages between peak open chromatin (ATAC) and gene expression (RNA) using regression model with covariates - Flexible specification of analysis using cells from a given cell type of interest, each cell type or all cells  Detailed information is available at GitHub ( https://github.com/hbliu/Open4Gene )
steps_raw:
Open4Gene (v1.0.0) is a hurdle model-based method for peak-to-gene linkage analysis in the single cell mutiome dataset. 
 Main features of Open4Gene - Accounting for excess zeros in single-nucleus RNA data based on a two-component mixture Hurdle model - Modeling linkages between peak open chromatin (ATAC) and gene expression (RNA) using regression model with covariates - Flexible specification of analysis using cells from a given cell type of interest, each cell type or all cells  Detailed information is available at GitHub ( https://github.com/hbliu/Open4Gene )
